get.summary: List all sleds known to wicketd and their bootstrap-network addresses

post.summary: Clear update state for one or more service processors
post.description: Use this to reset update state after a completed or failed update. This fails if any of the targeted service processors are currently being updated. Clearing targets that have no update data succeeds silently.

get.summary: Get inventory of all service processors visible to wicketd
get.description: If `force_refresh` is non-empty, the request will wait for the listed SPs to report fresh data, or return a 503 if they do not respond within the server-side timeout.

get.summary: Report the physical location (switch and sled) wicketd is running at
get.description: Will return 503 if the location is not yet known (typically because wicketd hasn't been able to connect to MGS).

get.summary: Query the current state of rack setup

post.description: Returns an error if the rack setup configuration has not been fully populated.

put.summary: Update (a subset of) the current RSS configuration
put.description: Sensitive values (certificates and the recovery password hash) are not set through this endpoint.

delete.summary: Reset all RSS configuration to default values

post.description: A certificate must be paired with its private key; the two may be posted in either order. Posting a certificate again before the pair completes replaces the pending certificate. Re-uploading a certificate and key that have already been accepted is detected as a duplicate: nothing is stored, and the response is `cert_key_duplicate_ignored`. If validation of a completed cert/key pair fails, both pending halves are discarded and must be re-posted.

post.summary: Add the private key of an external certificate
post.description: A private key must be paired with its certificate; the two may be posted in either order. Posting a key again before the pair completes replaces the pending key. Re-uploading a certificate and key that have already been accepted is detected as a duplicate: nothing is stored, and the response is `cert_key_duplicate_ignored`. If validation of a completed cert/key pair fails, both pending halves are discarded and must be re-posted.

put.summary: Set the recovery-silo user password hash

get.summary: Describe the TUF repository currently held by wicketd

put.summary: Upload a TUF repository to wicketd
put.description: At any given time, wicketd holds at most one TUF repository, extracted to ephemeral storage; any previously-uploaded repository is discarded. This request is rejected with a 400 while any service-processor update is in progress. A successful upload clears all update progress state, so a subsequent `GET /update-progress` returns an empty list.

post.summary: Start updating one or more service processors
post.description: A target that already has update state (even from a successful update) is rejected until that state is cleared with `/clear-update-state` or a new repository is uploaded.

get.summary: Report the update progress of every service processor with update state

AllowedSourceIps.description: Description of source IPs allowed to reach rack services.

oneOf.description: Allow traffic from any external IP address.

Restrict access to a specific set of source IP addresses or subnets.

All others are prevented from reaching rack services.

BgpAuthKeyId.description: The key identifier for authentication to use with a BGP peer.

asn.description: The autonomous system number for the BGP configuration.

checker.description: Checker to apply to incoming messages.

max_paths.description: Maximum number of paths to use when multiple "best paths" exist

originate.description: The set of prefixes for the BGP router to originate.

shaper.description: Shaper to apply to outgoing messages.

BootstrapSled.description:
A sled as seen on the bootstrap network.

A sled is reported here once its service processor's state has been read from MGS. (A populated cubby whose state has not yet been polled is absent until it is.)

A sled's `ip` becomes `Some` once it has been discovered on the bootstrap network; sleds still missing an address report `None`.

id.description: The service processor for this sled (its type and slot).

ip.description: The sled's bootstrap-network address, once it has been discovered.

Caboose.description:
A minimal projection of a firmware caboose.

Only the fields the commissioning client displays are included; the full caboose (git commit, sign, epoch, and so on) is intentionally omitted.

board.description: The board name the firmware was built for.

CertificateUploadResponse.description: The result of uploading half of a certificate/key pair.

oneOf.description: The key has been uploaded, but we're waiting on its corresponding certificate chain.
The cert chain has been uploaded, but we're waiting on its corresponding private key.
A cert chain and its key have been accepted.
A cert chain and its key are valid, but have already been uploaded.

targets.description: The service processors to clear update state for.

IgnitionFaults.description: The faults ignition reports for a present service processor.

Ipv4Net.title: An IPv4 subnet
Ipv4Net.description: An IPv4 subnet, including prefix and prefix length

Ipv4Range.description:
A non-decreasing IPv4 address range, inclusive of both ends.

The first address must be less than or equal to the last address.

Ipv6Net.title: An IPv6 subnet
Ipv6Net.description: An IPv6 subnet, including prefix and subnet mask

Ipv6Range.description:
A non-decreasing IPv6 address range, inclusive of both ends.

The first address must be less than or equal to the last address.

LinkFec.description: The forward error correction mode of a link.

LldpAdminStatus.description: To what extent should this port participate in LLDP

LldpPortConfig.description: Per-port LLDP configuration settings.  Only the "status" setting is mandatory.  All other fields have natural defaults or may be inherited from the switch.

chassis_id.description: Chassis ID to advertise.  If this is set, it will be advertised as a LocallyAssigned ID type.  If this is not set, it will be inherited from the switch-level settings.

management_addrs.description: Management IP addresses to advertise.  If this is not set, it will be inherited from the switch-level settings.

port_description.description: Port description to advertise.  If this is not set, no description will be advertised.

port_id.description: Port ID to advertise.  If this is set, it will be advertised as a LocallyAssigned ID type.  If this is not set, it will be set to the port name. e.g., qsfp0/0.

status.description: To what extent should this port participate in LLDP

system_description.description: System description to advertise.  If this is not set, it will be inherited from the switch-level settings.

system_name.description: System name to advertise.  If this is not set, it will be inherited from the switch-level settings.

LocationInfo.description: The physical location of the sled wicketd is running on.

sled_serial.description: The serial number of the sled wicketd is running on, if known.

switch_serial.description: The serial number of that switch's service processor, if known.

switch_slot.description: The slot of the switch this sled is cabled to.

ManualPortConfig.description:
User-specified per-port configuration.

This contains all of the fields of a port configuration other than the port name, which is used as the map key.

bgp_peers.description: BGP peers reachable on this port.

tx_eq.description: Transmit equalization overrides for this port.

uplink_port_fec.description: The forward error correction mode, if any.

Name.title: A name unique within the parent collection
Name.description: Names must begin with a lower case ASCII letter, be composed exclusively of lowercase ASCII, uppercase ASCII, numbers, and '-', and may not end with a '-'. Names cannot be a UUID, but they may contain a UUID. They can be at most 63 characters long.

PowerState.description:
See RFD 81.

This enum only lists power states the SP is able to control; higher power states are controlled by ignition.

PutRecoveryUserPasswordHash.description: The body of a request to set the recovery-silo user password hash.

hash.description: The password hash, in PHC string format.

PutRssUserConfigInsensitive.description:
The portion of the RSS configuration that can be posted in one shot.

It is provided by the operator uploading a TOML file. Sensitive values (certificates and the recovery password hash) are set separately.

allowed_source_ips.description: IPs or subnets allowed to make requests to user-facing services.

bootstrap_sleds.description:
The slot numbers of the sleds to bring up during RSS.

wicketd maps these back to sleds with the correct identifiers based on the bootstrap sleds it reports.

external_dns_ips.description: Service IP addresses on which external DNS servers are run.

external_dns_zone_name.description: The DNS zone name delegated to the rack for external DNS.

internal_services_ip_pool_ranges.description: Ranges of the service IP pool which may be used for internal services.

RackOperationStatus.description: The current status of any rack-level operation being performed.

id.description: The ID of the initialization operation.

oneOf.description: The rack is initialized. `id` is `None` if the rack was already initialized on startup.

id.description: The ID of the initialization operation, if one was performed.
The ID of the initialization operation.
The ID of the initialization operation.
The ID of the reset operation.

oneOf.description: The rack is uninitialized. `reset_id` is `None` if it was uninitialized on startup, or `Some` if a reset operation completed.

reset_id.description: The ID of the reset operation, if one was performed.

id.description: The ID of the reset operation.
The ID of the reset operation.

RepositoryDescription.description: A description of the TUF repository currently held by wicketd.

system_version.description: The system version of the uploaded TUF repository, if one is present.

RotInfo.description: Root-of-trust information for a single service processor.

caboose_a.description: The caboose of the image in slot A.

caboose_b.description: The caboose of the image in slot B.

caboose_stage0.description: The caboose of the stage0 bootloader.

caboose_stage0next.description: The caboose of the pending stage0next bootloader.

rib_priority.description: The RIB priority (i.e. Admin Distance) associated with this route.

vlan_id.description: The VLAN id associated with this route.

RouterLifetimeConfig.description: Router lifetime in seconds for unnumbered BGP peers

RssStepInfo.description: Information about the current RSS step.

description.description: A human-readable description of the current step.

step.description: The 1-based index of the current step.

total_steps.description: The total number of RSS steps.

RunningProgress.description: The most recent progress reported by a running step.

oneOf.description: The step is running but has not reported progress yet.
The step is running but has stalled without reporting further progress.
The step reported a progress counter.

SlotCaboose.description: The caboose for a single firmware slot, including whether it was read.

oneOf.description: The caboose for this slot has not been read yet.
The caboose for this slot was read.

SpIgnitionInfo.description: The ignition state of a service processor.

oneOf.description: Ignition reports the service processor as present.

faults.description: The faults ignition reports for the service processor.

power.description: Whether the service processor is powered on.

oneOf.description: Ignition reports the service processor as absent.

caboose_active.description: The caboose of the active service-processor firmware slot.

caboose_inactive.description: The caboose of the inactive service-processor firmware slot.

id.description: Identifies the service processor by type and slot.

ignition.description:
The ignition state of this service processor.

`None` means ignition state has not been read yet.

rot.description: Root-of-trust information, if it has been read.

state.description: The service processor's state, if it has been read.

mgs_last_seen.description:
How long it has been since wicketd last heard from MGS.

A large value indicates that MGS might be down.

SpInventoryParams.description: Parameters for the SP inventory endpoint.

force_refresh.description: Refresh the state of these service processors from MGS before returning, rather than returning their cached state. Service processors not listed here are returned from the cache.

SpStateInfo.description:
The service processor's state, read together from MGS.

The serial number and power state are always read together from the same service-processor state, so they are presented as a single unit that is either entirely present or entirely absent.

SpUpdateProgress.description:
Update progress for a single service processor.

An SP's update progress is only provided once an update has been started for it.

progress.description: The update progress for that service processor.

sp.description: The service processor this progress describes.

Stage0Caboose.description:
The stage0 (or pending stage0next) bootloader caboose for a root of trust.

The three states are kept distinct because they mean different things to a caller:

* `unsupported`: this RoT version does not report a stage0 bootloader caboose at all. * `not_read`: the RoT version supports reporting a stage0 caboose, but it has not been read yet. * `read`: the stage0 caboose was read.

oneOf.description: This RoT version does not report a stage0 bootloader caboose.
The stage0 bootloader caboose is supported but has not been read yet.
The stage0 bootloader caboose was read.

StartUpdateOptions.description: Options controlling how an update is performed.

skip_rot_bootloader_version_check.description: If true, skip the check on the current RoT bootloader version and always update it regardless of whether the update appears to be needed.

skip_rot_version_check.description: If true, skip the check on the current RoT version and always update it regardless of whether the update appears to be needed.

skip_sp_version_check.description: If true, skip the check on the current SP version and always update it regardless of whether the update appears to be needed.

StepOutcome.description: The outcome of a step that ran to completion.

oneOf.description: The step succeeded, optionally with a message.

message.description: An optional message describing the success.

oneOf.description: The step succeeded but produced a warning.

message.description: The message describing why the step was skipped.

total.description: The total value this progress counts towards, if known.

TxEqConfig.description: Per-port tx-eq overrides.  This can be used to fine-tune the transceiver equalization settings to improve signal integrity.

UpdateProgress.description: The progress of a single update execution: its overall state and its steps.

state.description: The overall rollup state of this execution.

steps.description: The steps of this execution, in order.

UpdateState.description: The state of an update execution.

oneOf.description: An update has been started, but execution has not begun yet.

The update ran to completion.

Whether individual steps performed work or were skipped (for example, because a component was already at its target version) is recorded in the per-step outcomes.

message.description: A human-readable description of the failure, combining the failed step and its error message (including any nested causes).
A human-readable description of the abort, combining the aborted step and its message.

UpdateStep.description:
A single node in the update step tree.

A step may spawn nested executions (for example, a per-component update that runs its own engine). Those executions appear in `children`.

children.description: The nested executions this step spawned, in order. Empty if the step ran no nested engine.

description.description: A human-readable description of this step.

UpdateStepStatus.description: The status of a single step in the update step tree.

oneOf.description: The step has not started yet.

progress.description: The most recent progress reported by the step.

outcome.description: The outcome of the completed step.

causes.description: The chain of underlying causes for the failure, outermost first.

message.description: A human-readable description of the failure.

oneOf.description: The step was aborted while running.

message.description: A human-readable description of the abort.

oneOf.description: The step will not be run because a prior step failed or was aborted.

reason.description: A human-readable description of why the step will not be run, naming the causal step.

UpdateTargets.description: A non-empty set of service processors to act on.

UserSpecifiedBgpPeerConfig.description:
User-specified configuration for a BGP peer.

This is similar to the internal BGP peer configuration, except it does not carry the sensitive `md5_auth_key`; the operator provides the key separately, referenced by `auth_key_id`.

allowed_export.description: Apply export policy to this peer with an allow list.

allowed_import.description: Apply import policy to this peer with an allow list.

asn.description: The autonomous system number of the router the peer belongs to.

auth_key_id.description: The key identifier for authentication to use with the peer.

communities.description: Include the provided communities in updates sent to the peer.

connect_retry.description: The interval in seconds between peer connection retry attempts. Defaults to 3 seconds.

delay_open.description: How long to delay sending open messages to a peer, in seconds. Defaults to 0.

enforce_first_as.description: Enforce that the first AS in paths received from this peer is the peer's AS.

hold_time.description: How long to keep a session alive without a keepalive, in seconds. Defaults to 6 seconds.

idle_hold_time.description: How long to keep a peer in idle after a state machine reset, in seconds. Defaults to 3 seconds.

keepalive.description: The interval to send keepalive messages at, in seconds. Defaults to 2 seconds.

local_pref.description: Apply a local preference to routes received from this peer.

min_ttl.description: Require messages from a peer have a minimum IP time to live field.

multi_exit_discriminator.description: Apply the provided multi-exit discriminator (MED) updates sent to the peer.

port.description: Switch port the peer is reachable on.

remote_asn.description: Require that a peer has a specified ASN.

router_lifetime.description: Router lifetime in seconds for unnumbered BGP peers.

vlan_id.description: Associate a VLAN ID with a BGP peer session.

anyOf.description: A port configured automatically via DDM.

UserSpecifiedRackNetworkConfig.description: User-specified parts of the rack network configuration.

infra_ip_first.description: The first address of the infrastructure IP range.

infra_ip_last.description: The last address of the infrastructure IP range.

rack_subnet_address.description: The rack subnet address, if statically assigned.

switch0.description: Per-port configuration for switch 0, keyed by port name.

switch1.description: Per-port configuration for switch 1, keyed by port name.

UserSpecifiedUplinkAddressConfig.description:
A user-specified uplink address configuration.

This provides a friendlier TOML representation of an uplink address.

address.description: The address to be used on the uplink.

vlan_id.description: The VLAN id (if any) associated with this address.
